problem_addressed: Simulation-based inference methods require large numbers of samples and struggle with high-dimensional settings, making inference prohibitive for resource-intensive simulators.
core_claim: ASNPE improves sample efficiency by integrating active learning into the inference loop to estimate the utility of simulation parameter candidates.
key_contributions: Introduction of active sequential neural posterior estimation (ASNPE) with active learning scheme
Acquisition function that balances epistemic uncertainty and likelihood in parameter selection
Demonstration of effectiveness in high-dimensional travel demand calibration problem
novelty: Active learning scheme integrated into SBI pipeline to select informative simulation parameters, unlike non-active counterparts that sample uniformly or heuristically.
method_summary: ASNPE sequentially trains neural density estimators while selecting parameters that maximize expected divergence between posterior estimates, using an acquisition function that targets epistemic uncertainty.
method_components: Acquisition function α(θ, p(ϕ|D)) = ˜p(θ) · E[ (p(θ|x₀, D) - p(θ|x₀, ϕ))² ]
Sequential training of NDEs via APT with proposal distributions ˜p(r)(θ)
Optional MC-dropout for approximating Bayesian model parameter posterior p(ϕ|D)
key_innovation: Acquisition function that selects parameters maximizing expected squared divergence between marginal and conditional posteriors, targeting epistemic uncertainty in the NDE model.
assumptions: NDE can be trained via maximum likelihood using Approximate Posterior Transformation (APT)
Proposal distributions connect to prior support covering entire parameter domain
MC-dropout provides consistent sampling for Bayesian posterior approximation (when used)
theoretical_grounding: Proposition that HD_KL = I[ϕ; θ|x₀, D] (mutual information) with proof in Appendix A.1; convergence guarantees as N → ∞ for qϕ(θ|x) → p(θ|x)
datasets_used: common SBI benchmark environments (Bernoulli GLM, SLCP)
hyperparameters: maximum of 128 SUMO simulations, batch update every 32 simulation draws, 5 repeated simulation runs
seeds: 5 repeated simulation runs (number of seeds not explicitly stated)
headline_result: ASNPE outperforms other methods across most settings with better RMSNE scores using fewer simulations
improvement_over_baseline: ASNPE reaches better RMSNE scores than SNPE and ABC with fewer simulations
stated_limitations: Many simulation-based inference methods require large numbers of simulation samples
Methods face difficulty scaling to high-dimensional settings
Inference can be prohibitive under resource-intensive simulators
unstated_weaknesses: Method designed for simulation-based inference may not translate directly to medical image classification where forward model is neural network
May not address specific challenges of medical imaging like class imbalance and domain shifts
Unclear how method handles interpretability requirements in medical applications
threats_to_validity: Domain mismatch between simulation-based inference and medical imaging applications
Potential lack of calibration-specific metrics in evaluation
Unclear performance on medical image classification tasks
reproducibility: medium (affiliated with Google Research and USC but no explicit reproducibility information provided)
open_questions: How would ASNPE perform on medical image classification tasks?
Can the active learning strategy be adapted for medical imaging challenges?
How does the method ensure calibration of posterior estimates in medical applications?
future_work: Extending method to more complex simulators and higher-dimensional problems
Applying approach to scientific domains with simulation-based inference
gaps_this_enables: Active learning framework for sample-efficient inference adaptable to medical imaging
Framework for reducing labeled data requirements in medical image classification
New approaches for calibration-aware active learning in medical imaging
relevance_to_topic: This paper introduces an active learning approach for improving sample efficiency in inference that could be adapted for calibration-aware active learning in medical image classification.
best_result: ASNPE outperforms other methods across most settings with better RMSNE scores using fewer simulations
limitations_stated: Many simulation-based inference methods require large numbers of simulation samples
Methods face difficulty scaling to high-dimensional settings
Inference can be prohibitive under resource-intensive simulators
title: Active Sequential Posterior Estimation for Sample-Efficient Simulation-Based Inference
